title: PostgreSQL 18 Domain Schema, Keys, Constraints, and Index Intent · Language Lens: Feed Go Stable Keys
summary: The database uses URLs as mutable primary keys, repeats publisher strings, permits cross-tenant references, lacks temporal constraints, and indexes without query evidence. Predict the reader decision, feed or API contract, Go and PostgreSQL ownership, bounded failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed go stable keys increment in constrained relational feed-service schema. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory,…
objectives: Use stable internal keys plus scoped external identities and preserve canonical URL changes without breaking references.
Model tenants, users, sources, feeds, subscriptions, entries, authors, categories, content versions, fetches, and reader state with clear ownership.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Predict Feed Go Stable Keys · Theory Feed Go Stable Keys
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 1144 during an accessible community-news reader review. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile feed-service entities, database invariants, tenant keys, and query-driven indexes; publisher fetch and retention budgets must remain explicit; then interleave one schedule, lease, fencing, goroutine, cancellation, or shutdown path and prove ownership. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using stable and internal plus specific case evidence.
